documentation_accuracy_summary: include/strategic/FPlanet.h's Doxygen on load() (lines 70-95) accurately and completely documents the per-read checking discipline (m_ID, m_name, stationCount, station type tag), the createShip() NULL-check-before-use guard, the nested m_station->load(is) check, and the full FSystem::load()/FMap::load()/FGame::load() propagation-and-cleanup chain -- independently verified against the actual FSystem::load() (src/strategic/FSystem.cpp:92-121, which does delete p and return 1 on p->load(is)!=0) and FMap::load() (src/strategic/FMap.cpp:340-381, which does delete s and return 1 on s->load(is)!=0) source. @author (Tom Stephens, Claude Sonnet 5 (medium)) and @date Last Modified (Jul 19, 2026) correctly updated per project convention, with the original author preserved.

AGENTS.md's save-format Contributor Notes paragraph (Documenter edit) accurately describes the FF2-2/FR-C fix and correctly states it hardens FPlanet::load() 'the same way' as the prior FF-2/FVehicle::load() fix, closing what was previously a NULL virtual-call crash; the Testing section's strategic-tests bullet accurately names the new coverage at both the FGameSaveFormatTest full-game level and the FPlanetTest unit level, and correctly states the rationale for the truncated-station-record scenario being unit-level-only -- consistent with the code and with my own independent trace of that reasoning.

No duplication or contradiction found between the FPlanet.h Doxygen, the inline src/strategic/FPlanet.cpp comments (which correctly describe the ~FPlanet()-frees-partial-m_station-via-not-yet-owned-planet-deletion ownership chain), and the AGENTS.md paragraph -- each is calibrated to its audience and the facts are mutually consistent and independently confirmed true against the actual source.
doc/deferred-tasks.md correctly left unchanged, matching the plan's explicit statement that FR-C was a reviewer follow-up rather than a tracked deferred item.
test_sufficiency_summary: Sufficient and behavioral, matching the repo's behavioral-test mandate. FPlanetTest's 8 new tests each build a real FPlanet::save() byte stream via the savePlanetBytes() helper, truncate or corrupt it at a precise, arithmetically-justified offset, call the real FPlanet::load(std::istream&) in isolation, and assert on observed runtime outcomes (return code, getStation()==NULL, getName()/getStation()->getType() preserved) -- not source-text shape.

testLoadReturnsNonzeroOnUnknownStationType (tests/strategic/FPlanetTest.cpp:179-213) is the memory-safety-critical case: it overwrites only the station's type-name bytes with a same-length unrecognized string so createShip(type) returns NULL, then asserts load() returns nonzero AND getStation()==NULL, with reaching the assertion itself proving no NULL virtual-call crash occurred. I independently confirmed this is fix-discriminating by reverting src/strategic/FPlanet.cpp to the pre-fix version and running SSWTests directly: the process segfaulted (exit 139) rather than merely failing an assertion, which is a stronger and more convincing demonstration of the crash-class regression than an assertion failure alone.

testLoadReturnsNonzeroOnTruncatedStationRecord (tests/strategic/FPlanetTest.cpp:215-237) truncates 2 bytes into the nested station's own m_ID field (verified against FVehicle::load()'s actual leading readU32(is,id) at src/ships/FVehicle.cpp:216-224) after a fully-intact, consumed type tag, isolating the fix from the createShip()==NULL guard and exercising the m_station->load(is) return-check specifically.

Two positive controls (testLoadValidSaveWithStationReturnsZeroAndPreservesData, testLoadValidSaveWithoutStationReturnsZeroAndPreservesData) assert rc==0 and preserved name/station-type data, proving no false rejection on the happy path.

FGameSaveFormatTest::testLoadPlanetWithUnknownStationTypeReturnsNonzeroAndReportsExactlyOnce (tests/strategic/FGameSaveFormatTest.cpp:866-909) is a genuine full-game integration test: it locates and corrupts the real 'Fortress Kdikit' station's type tag inside a full buildValidSaveBytes() save (via byte-offset arithmetic verified against FVehicle::save()'s actual field order), calls the real FGame::load(std::istream&), and asserts rc!=0, exactly one RecordingUI::showMessageCalls, and g.getPlayers().size()==0 -- proving the abort fires via FMap::load()'s nested FSystem::load()/FPlanet::load() chain before any player is committed to the live singleton.

The Tester's decision to omit an analogous full-game truncated-station-record test is a correct, independently-re-verified judgment call, not a coverage gap -- see Findings/NOTE above for the full chain-of-reads trace confirming the full-game scenario cannot discriminate the fix (both fixed and unfixed code abort nonzero, for different reasons: the fix aborts at FPlanet::load() itself; unfixed code's failure is masked locally but still caught by FMap::load()'s next checked read against the already-failed stream).

Independently re-confirmed the behavioral-test mandate myself end-to-end: reverted src/strategic/FPlanet.cpp to 7261e534^, rebuilt (make all_clean + tests make), and ran SSWTests directly -- it segfaulted (exit 139) partway through the strategic suite, consistent with testLoadReturnsNonzeroOnUnknownStationType's NULL virtual-call path being exercised on unfixed code. Restored the fixed file (git diff --stat produced no output afterward, confirming byte-for-byte restoration), rebuilt via make all_clean + make check, and observed a fully green run.

No production file outside src/strategic/FPlanet.cpp and include/strategic/FPlanet.h was touched by the Implementer; no production file was touched by the Tester -- confirmed via diff inspection (tests/strategic/{FPlanetTest,FGameSaveFormatTest}.{h,cpp} and AGENTS.md are the only other changed files).
